title: Equational Theories: Equation 255 does not imply Equation 677 (class-free restatement)
informal_statement: From the Equational Theories project (Tao et al.): Equation 255 (the identity x = ((x*x)*x)*x for a binary operation *) does not imply Equation 677 (the identity x = y*(x*((y*x)*y))). Restated class-free, avoiding the upstream `Magma` typeclass (which is purely notational sugar around a binary op that the proof never pattern-matches as a class): there exists a binary operation op on Fin 3 satisfying Equation 255 identically but not Equation 677.
informal_proof_idea: Explicit finite witness: op i j = i + j + 1 (mod 3) on Fin 3, matching the upstream operation table [[1,2,0],[2,0,1],[0,1,2]]. Equation 255 holds by case-check on all 3 elements (fin_cases + decide); Equation 677 fails by direct decidable evaluation (decide finds a counterexample pair).
formal_statement_pp: theorem equational_theories_677_255_class_free : ∃ op : Fin 3 → Fin 3 → Fin 3, (∀ x : Fin 3, x = op (op (op x x) x) x) ∧ ¬ (∀ x y : Fin 3, x = op y (op x (op (op y x) y)))

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this is a RESTATEMENT of the upstream Equation255_not_implies_Equation677 result avoiding the Magma typeclass layer, not a verbatim transport of the upstream proof term -- the upstream proof uses `class Magma (alpha) where op : alpha -> alpha -> alpha` plus `abbrev`s for Equation255/Equation677, which SubmitModule cannot transport (LeanModuleItem has no inductive/class/structure item kind; see packets/frontier/formal_conjectures/BLOCKERS.md). This packet's statement was checked by hand to be definitionally identical to the upstream one after unfolding Magma.op/Equation255/Equation677 (the class is purely notational sugar; the upstream proof never pattern-matches Magma as a class, only ever uses Magma.op as a plain function) -- this is a genuine, previously-solved literature result (Equational Theories project, Tao et al.), NOT an open problem, and this packet does not resolve or touch the file's actual open conjectures (Finite.Equation677_not_implies_Equation255, Finite.Equation677_implies_Equation255, still sorry upstream). Found via a concurrent agent's triage recorded in packets/frontier/formal_conjectures/SOLVED_QUEUE.md (round 3, commit 7456692) as a ready-to-attempt candidate; this packet executes that sketch through the tracked proof-search loop end to end (problem_create -> episode_create -> attempt_claim -> episode_step, kernel_verified on the first Solve attempt). training.eligibility: quarantined out of caution (consistent with this session's default-quarantine policy for adapted/restated frontier packets pending real external review), even though open_problem_related is false for this specific theorem -- first packet authored in the formal_conjectures lane, so no established public_train precedent exists yet for this lane's solved-upstream category.
